description: Leaky-bucket rate limiting backed by distributed in-memory storage, with push-challenge and CAPTCHA-challenge flows that restore access for legitimate users when limits are exceeded

validation.message: Bucket size must be at least 1

given: leaky bucket has sufficient permits for the requested amount after accounting for the current fill level and elapsed time

then.description: Atomically deduct permits from the bucket and record the current timestamp

permit_granted.result: Request proceeds normally; no response header added

given: leaky bucket deficit after deduction is greater than zero
permit_denied.result: Request rejected with 429 Too Many Requests and Retry-After header indicating the calculated wait

given: account has a registered push token on its primary device
push challenge issuance is permitted by the per-request constraint checker

then.description: Generate a 16-byte random token, persist it with a 5-minute TTL, and dispatch it to the primary device via push notification

push_challenge_sent.result: Push notification with the challenge token dispatched to the account's primary device

given: account has no registered push token on its primary device
push_challenge_no_token.result: 404 returned; client must register a push token before requesting a push challenge

given: submitted token matches the stored token for this account and has not expired
push challenge attempt rate limiter has permits
push challenge success rate limiter has permits
rate limit reset frequency limiter has permits

then.description: Consume and delete the one-time token
Reset the relevant leaky-bucket rate limiters for the account

push_challenge_verified.result: Challenge accepted; associated rate limiters reset; client may retry the original request

given: submitted push challenge token does not match the stored token or has expired
push_challenge_invalid.result: Challenge silently rejected (token not consumed); rate limiters remain in effect

given: external CAPTCHA service validates the solution as genuine at or above the configured score threshold
captcha challenge attempt rate limiter has permits
captcha challenge success rate limiter has permits
rate limit reset frequency limiter has permits

then.description: Reset the relevant leaky-bucket rate limiters for the account

captcha_challenge_verified.result: CAPTCHA accepted; associated rate limiters reset; client may retry the original request

given: external CAPTCHA service rejects the solution or the score is below the configured threshold
captcha_challenge_failed.result: Request rejected with 428 Precondition Required; client must obtain a valid CAPTCHA solution

given: rate limit reset frequency limiter for this account is exhausted
rate_limit_reset_exhausted.result: Request rejected with 429 Too Many Requests; challenge farming is blocked

errors.message: Request rate limit exceeded. Please wait before retrying.
No push token registered for this device. Please update your push registration and try again.
CAPTCHA verification failed. Please try again.
Too many rate limit resets. Please wait before attempting another challenge.

events.name: rate_limit.exceeded
events.description: A request was rejected because the leaky-bucket rate limiter had insufficient permits

events.name: rate_limit.push_challenge_sent
events.description: A push challenge notification was dispatched to the account's primary device

events.name: rate_limit.challenge_answered
events.description: A challenge (push or CAPTCHA) was successfully verified and rate limiters were reset

events.name: rate_limit.challenge_failed
events.description: A submitted challenge response was invalid

events.name: rate_limit.reset_limit_exceeded
events.description: An account attempted to reset rate limits via challenge too frequently

related.reason: Push challenges are delivered to devices via the push notification subsystem
Leaky-bucket state is stored in a distributed in-memory cache with atomic Lua scripting
Authentication endpoints are typically among the first to be rate-limited

goals.description: Leaky-bucket rate limiting backed by distributed in-memory storage, with push-challenge and CAPTCHA-challenge flows that restore access for legitimate users when limits are exceeded

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Failed operations divided by total attempts

constraints.description: Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

tradeoffs.reason: infrastructure downtime impacts all dependent services

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
